goals.g1: Record the implemented check_isomorphism_anchored and its two-case test: positive case (genuine match) returns reversible:True with a confirmed anchor; negative case (deliberately corrupted retained layer) correctly returns reversible:False with the exact edge mismatch, confirming the check can genuinely fail, not just default to True.

prep_note: Implements ers_isomorphism_check.json's committed design. Prepared before recording results.

prior_candidates.statement: Implemented in basic_machinery/reverse_compound.py: build_reversed_operation (wraps build_reversed_graph2+compile_schema, cached), collect_reversed_matches (phase 1, blind match_all per rule, flags per-rule multiplicity), resolve_reverse_compound (phase 2, OV_forward vs OV_reverse structural comparison via detect_overlaps reused unmodified twice -- no ordering, no enumeration, per this session's correction). Verified end-to-end against a single-rule synthetic case using an explicit-category rule. NOT verified: any genuine multi-rule compound firing (no test built); footprint_is_consistent is still not wired to a real apply_compound aggregate; the full isomorphism-to-retained-previous-layer check is not implemented at all -- resolve_reverse_compound only gets as far as the OV comparison. A real, uncorrected defect was found and left uncorrected (root_cause_and_rule_misapplied): reversed rules built from a forward rule using ph_all_four (blanket, variable-presence preservation) will incorrectly find zero matches against real firings where fewer than all 4 declared categories are actually present, because the exact-degree matcher cannot express 'however many of these are present'. This affects any real rule using ph_all_four (at least spine_finalise_v1.py's out_rspine, confirmed earlier this session) and is carried forward, not fixed.

derived_from.rule: state plainly what is verified working, what is unverified, and what is a known, real, uncorrected defect -- not overclaiming completeness

goals: Implement the corrected reversibility check design (per ers_lf6_repair.json and ers_compose_reversed_compound.json's surviving structure): collect_reversed_matches(matches, result_graph) -- per-rule blind match_all of each reversed rule against the result, flagging per-rule multiplicity; resolve_reverse_compound -- compare OV_forward (detect_overlaps on the known forward matches/base graph) against OV_reverse (detect_overlaps on the reverse matches/result graph), no enumeration, no ordering; reverse_fire -- thin orchestrator wiring these to footprint_is_consistent (existing) and the isomorphism check.

prior_candidates.statement: Implemented in reverse_compound.py: reverse_footprint_lightweight (coarse S_reverse_implied, over-approximates by including ALL survived positions not just changed ones -- explicitly documented as capable of producing false mismatches, not swept under) and reverse_fire (orchestrator: collect_reversed_matches -> per-rule-multiplicity check -> resolve_reverse_compound -> footprint_is_consistent -> stops, correctly refusing to claim 'reversible: True' since the isomorphism check does not exist). Tested end to end for the first time: using rebuild()'s output_map as a stand-in S_forward (no apply_compound/LayeredGraph adapter exists, per imported_layered_graph_integration_needed), the full pipeline ran, footprint and overlap checks both passed, and the result correctly reports 'unresolved' rather than 'reversible: True' -- verified by actual execution, not asserted.

derived_from.rule: implement per the committed design, test against the same single-rule case already exercised this session, and report exactly what passed and what remains explicitly unresolved

goals: Record the reverse_fire orchestrator implementation and its first end-to-end test result, including the honest scope limits (coarse footprint approximation, no-overlap-only, no isomorphism check, stand-in S_forward since no apply_compound/LayeredGraph adapter exists).

prior_candidates.statement: ADOPTED: cand_bfs_direct_comparison, WITH the falsifier's correction incorporated: (1) build the candidate reconstruction via rebuild(result_graph.copy(), reversed_op.schema.edits, reversed_binding) -- reusing the proven mechanism, no new materialization logic. (2) Pick anchor from the CONFIRMED-unchanged set (not from an arbitrary match_all binding). (3) BFS-grow a correspondence from the anchor: for INHERITED/confirmed-unchanged real nodes, compare incident edges by DIRECT real-id equality (same physical node, by construction). For BORN/reconstructed nodes (freshly minted, no shared id with base_graph possible), compare STRUCTURALLY instead -- find whether some node in base_graph, adjacent to the already-confirmed correspondence, has matching edge structure relative to what's already anchored; require this structural match, not id equality, for born positions specifically. (4) Require full coverage: every node in both the reconstruction and base_graph must be reached by the BFS; unreached/disconnected nodes are a coverage gap, not a silent pass. Rejected: cand_seeded_match_all (failed its falsifier -- derive_match_view requires graph2-specific scaffolding a plain materialized graph doesn't have; a confirmed blocker, not an inconvenience).

derived_from.rule: adopt the candidate that survived its falsifier, incorporating the real correction it surfaced (born nodes need structural not id-based matching), and reject the one shown to have a concrete, confirmed blocker

goals: Implement the isomorphism check (refined_classifier's final, still-unbuilt piece): choose an anchor from a CONFIRMED-unchanged node (not from match_all's arbitrary resolution), use it to seed a structural correspondence, materialize ('build') the reconstructed candidate layer, and compare it against the retained previous layer (base_graph).

prior_candidates.statement: Re-ran the EXACT ph_all_four test case that previously found zero matches (imported_test_found_zero_matches). After implementing the physical-switch fix (imported_fix_design: build_reversed_graph2's confirmed_crossings parameter, collect_reversed_matches passing base_graph+forward binding through to _read_boundary_externals per firing): per_rule_multiplicity is now {0: 1} (was {0: 0}), exactly 1 reversed match found, resolve_reverse_compound reports consistent=True. Verified by actual execution against the identical rule and base graph that failed before, not a different/easier test case.

derived_from.rule: re-running the same failing case after implementing the designed fix and observing the failure resolve is direct empirical confirmation

goals: Verify and record the physical-switch fix's actual test result: does re-running the exact ph_all_four case that previously found zero matches now succeed?

prior_candidates.statement: Implemented compute_unchanged_candidates in reversed_rule.py (plus helpers _input_internal_edges, _node_spectrum): for each core.inherit pair (already guaranteed injective/bijective, no new work there), compares declared (type,direction) spectrum between input and output side, returning candidates only where they match EXACTLY. Tested against two cases: (1) the ph_all_four toy rule (output declares 4 categories, input declares 1) -- correctly returns EMPTY (spectra genuinely differ, not just presence). (2) an explicit single-category rule (both sides declare exactly (STRUCTURAL,'out')) -- correctly returns the (in_A, out_B) pair as a candidate. Both verified by actual execution.

derived_from.rule: implement per the committed combined design and verify both the negative (mismatch) and positive (match) case against real graph2 examples already used this session

goals: Record the compute_unchanged_candidates implementation and its two-case verification (spectrum mismatch correctly excluded for ph_all_four; spectrum match correctly included for the explicit single-category rule).

imported_sven_hints.statement: Sven (direct, this session): choose an anchor from an unchanged node -- not resolved via match, i.e. not derived from whatever binding match_all happened to produce -- and use one of its nodes as anchor. Build and compare the layer.

imported_rebuild_is_reusable_for_reconst.statement: rebuild(graph, edits, binding) (schema.py) applies ANY compiled schema's edits against ANY graph+binding -- it does not know or care whether the schema represents a 'forward' or 'reversed' operation. The reversed operation built by build_reversed_graph2/build_reversed_operation is a fully compiled OperationDefinition with its own .schema.edits. Therefore rebuild(result_graph.copy(), reversed_op.schema.edits, reversed_binding) can MATERIALIZE the candidate reconstruction by reusing the exact same, already-proven mechanism used for every forward firing test today -- no new materialization logic needs to be written.

imported_anchor_source_is_confirmed_unch.statement: The anchor must come from the CONFIRMED-unchanged set (compute_unchanged_candidates + per-firing confirmed crossings, already built in reverse_footprint_lightweight) -- NOT from an arbitrary node that match_all's binding happens to include. A confirmed-unchanged node's real id is verified identical in both base_graph and result_graph BY CONSTRUCTION (it survived with provably no change), which is exactly what makes it usable as a zero-search, known-correct seed for growing a correspondence -- an arbitrary matched node has no such guarantee.

imported_isomorphism_design.statement: ADOPTED: cand_bfs_direct_comparison, WITH the falsifier's correction incorporated: (1) build the candidate reconstruction via rebuild(result_graph.copy(), reversed_op.schema.edits, reversed_binding) -- reusing the proven mechanism, no new materialization logic. (2) Pick anchor from the CONFIRMED-unchanged set (not from an arbitrary match_all binding). (3) BFS-grow a correspondence from the anchor: for INHERITED/confirmed-unchanged real nodes, compare incident edges by DIRECT real-id equality (same physical node, by construction). For BORN/reconstructed nodes (freshly minted, no shared id with base_graph possible), compare STRUCTURALLY instead -- find whether some node in base_graph, adjacent to the already-confirmed correspondence, has matching edge structure relative to what's already anchored; require this structural match, not id equality, for born positions specifically. (4) Require full coverage: every node in both the reconstruction and base_graph must be reached by the BFS; unreached/disconnected nodes are a coverage gap, not a silent pass. Rejected: cand_seeded_match_all (failed its falsifier -- derive_match_view requires graph2-specific scaffolding a plain materialized graph doesn't have; a confirmed blocker, not an inconvenience).

derived_from.rule: adopt the candidate that survived its falsifier, incorporating the real correction it surfaced (born nodes need structural not id-based matching), and reject the one shown to have a concrete, confirmed blocker (imported, parents not re-grounded — external provenance)

implemented_and_both_tested.statement: Implemented check_isomorphism_anchored + _confirmed_unchanged_real_nodes in reverse_compound.py, wired into reverse_fire replacing the prior 'unresolved' placeholder. Tested TWO cases: (1) genuine match against the correct retained layer -- reversible:True, anchor=Node(0), empty born_correspondence (no born nodes in this toy rule). (2) the SAME setup against a DELIBERATELY CORRUPTED retained layer (an extra real edge added that never existed) -- reversible:False, reason='edge mismatch at confirmed node Node(0)', with the exact differing edge sets reported. This confirms the check can genuinely detect a real discrepancy, not merely default to True regardless of input.

derived_from.rule: implement per the committed design and verify BOTH a positive and a deliberately-engineered negative case before accepting the implementation as doing real work

chk_falsifier_implemented.method: does the implementation still carry the honestly-scoped limits from the design (multi-match not implemented, zero-anchor case reported distinctly), or did the code silently drop them during implementation?
chk_falsifier_implemented.result: Checked against the actual code: check_isomorphism_anchored explicitly returns {'reversible':'unverifiable', ...} for len(matches)!=1 (multi-match case) and separately for the zero-anchor case (no confirmed-unchanged node found) -- both distinct from True/False, matching the committed design exactly. Neither limit was dropped during implementation.

chk_relevance_implemented.method: connect the evidence to this session's stated goal
chk_relevance_implemented.result: Goal: record the implementation and its test results. Directly reports both, including the negative-case verification that confirms the check does real work.

committed.statement: Implemented check_isomorphism_anchored + _confirmed_unchanged_real_nodes in reverse_compound.py, wired into reverse_fire replacing the prior 'unresolved' placeholder. Tested TWO cases: (1) genuine match against the correct retained layer -- reversible:True, anchor=Node(0), empty born_correspondence (no born nodes in this toy rule). (2) the SAME setup against a DELIBERATELY CORRUPTED retained layer (an extra real edge added that never existed) -- reversible:False, reason='edge mismatch at confirmed node Node(0)', with the exact differing edge sets reported. This confirms the check can genuinely detect a real discrepancy, not merely default to True regardless of input.
